p: Having Model Engine 4 on the server.

Download the plugin .zip The zip contains a .jar and a folder with the Necessary Models. Place the models in the blueprints folder of ModelEngine. Use /meg reload to generate a resource pack. Load the texture pack
Install WorldGuard to use the avatar spawn denial feature in regions.

l: Once the plugin and its dependencies are installed:
p: Go to the point where you want the player to preview the avatars, Go to the location where the model will appear and type
Once you see the confirmation message in the chat, go to the location where you want the camera to be positioned (from where the player will view the models) and type

Once you see the confirmation message in the chat, you can be assured that these locations will be maintained (unless you decide to move them, for which you should place both commands again in the new locations)

l: Let's see that everything works correctly.
p: At this point, if you use the command You will see a menu with two options:

My Avatars, where all the avatars for which the player has permission will appear. (Refer to Permissions(page) to know the available permissions) If the player has the Avatar permission, it will appear in the form of a chest. By selecting it, you will see the option to activate/deactivate the avatar (by default, all would come deactivated)
Preview Avatars, where all the avatars available on the server will appear, even if the player does not have permission.
the permission logoutavatars.selector.preview is necessary to preview the avatars, if you do not want the user to be able to do so, do not assign the permission.
Command
Description
This command will reload the configuration file, messages, and existing avatars.
This command allows the administration to assign a logout avatar via command. (Check if the player has the avatar permission)
This command allows the administration to set the camera location in the preview.
This command allows the administration to set the model location in the preview.
This command activates the spawning of your current avatar.
This command deactivates the spawning of your current avatar.
This command opens the avatar selection and preview menu.
🔽 Here you can enable or disable control messages in the console.

🔽 Here you can select the radius of the protection generated around the avatar. This protection is used to prevent the player from placing or breaking blocks within the region. (Interaction with blocks like doors or chests will still be possible)(Coming soon: protection radius per avatar)
🔽 Here you can set the WorldGuard regions where you want to prevent avatars from appearing. (Coming soon: disabled regions per avatar)
You can create your own avatars using Blockbench. Remember that you will then import it into the blueprints folder of Model Engine 4

l: What options do I have for creating the new Avatar?

p: 🔼This animation will be displayed when the Avatar appears.
🔼This animation will loop after the spawn animation.
🔼This animation will play once the Avatar's persistence time has expired.

l: Apply the player's skin to the model
p: To be able to apply the player's skin to a model, it is necessary to use tags in the model. Keep in mind that if the player uses Shaders, they will only see the head.
Player Limbs Tags.
🔽Here you should put the name that will appear to the player for the avatar.
🔽Here you should put the name of the model (la_beach_model.bbmodel).
🔽Coming soon (indicates whether the avatar requires using the player's skin or not).
🔽Here you should put the unit of time measurement in which the avatar should disappear (minutes/hours).
🔽The number of (minutes/hours) the avatar will wait before automatically despawning (0 to not despawn and wait for the player to reconnect).
🔽Time in seconds of the spawn animation defined in the .bbmodel.
🔽Time in seconds of the despawn animation defined in the .bbmodel.
🔽Hologram that will be displayed above the avatar.
🔽Height of the hologram's position.
🔽Permission to use the avatar. This permission will be added to logoutavatars.avatar.beach.
Remember that you can copy and paste an existing avatar and work based on it.
Permmision
Description
Permission for the command /la reload
Permission for the command /la set
Permission for the command /la setcam
Permission for the command /la setmodel
Permission for the command /la enable
Permission for the command /la disable
Permission for the command /la selector
Permission to preview avatars in the selector

Thank you very much for making it this far! I hope this plugin can give a fresh touch to your server. I'm here to give you some extra things, free of course. In our Discord, you will find a channel with the name of the plugin. There, I will be posting avatars that have been requested or that I come up with, periodically. I hope you find them useful!
